signal_evidence: Between 2022-10-07 and 2022-10-07, the customer conducted 1 transaction(s) — predominantly cross-border wires — amounting to approximately NPR 10,861.
Of these, 1 were cross-border, routed to counterparties outside the customer's usual corridor.
The principal counterparties observed were Oconnor and Sons.
In the reviewing officer's judgement the velocity and aggregate value of the funds are not fully consistent with that profile.
A number of the transfers appear to have been deliberately kept below reporting thresholds and were executed in rapid succession, a pattern the officer considers consistent with Layered Fan In.
noise_evidence: During the branch's periodic transaction-monitoring review, the compliance desk at NIMB examined the account held by Kimberly Aguirre after an internal threshold alert was triggered.
This report is submitted out of an abundance of caution for the compliance unit's further analysis and does not, by itself, constitute a determination of wrongdoing.

Logical reasoning linking facts to suspicion is present.
